message:
fix(validation): allow dots in ID prefixes and skip prefix check with --force

The ID format validator rejected prefixes containing dots (e.g.,
"jeffreys-skills.md-02h"), making it impossible to import JSONL files
with legacy multi-segment prefixes. This caused CONFIG_ERROR on all
br operations when such IDs existed in the JSONL.

- validation/mod.rs: Add '.' to allowed prefix characters
- sync.rs: --force flag now also skips prefix validation, enabling
  import of multi-prefix JSONL files without renaming

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -924,7 +924,7 @@ fn execute_import(
 
     // Configure import
     let import_config = ImportConfig {
-        skip_prefix_validation: false,
+        skip_prefix_validation: args.force,
         rename_on_import: false,
         clear_duplicate_external_refs: false,
         orphan_mode,
